responses.content: She is trying to convince people how we need to make a change in the world, how things can be not different from other things, we all have to treat everybody equally. And how she's saying from June 1st, we will reach the moon, is how she's trying to tell you how we have to keep on achieving and we always have to meet that goal.
responses.bucket_rationale: The response shows engagement with the text and mentions that Malala is trying to convince people to make a change and treat everyone equally. It also references the idea of achieving goals, like reaching the moon. The connection between space achievements and the achievability of universal education is not made explicit, so this is best categorized as partial-understanding.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best conveys the central idea of this text?

responses.content: C. After the adults in her community failed to accomplish any change, Barbara Johns led fellow African American students in a strike for better school conditions.

questions.question: Which sentence from the excerpt best supports the correct answer to Question 7?
